methods.description: This method describes the process of monitoring pollen tube growth in pistils after cross-pollination of Arachis species. It involves collecting pistils, fixing them in a solution, preparing them for microscopy, and analyzing the growth using fluorescence microscopy.

steps.description: Flowers were collected at time of anthesis and rubbed over the stigma of female parent that had been emasculated at previous day by removing the anthers and petals to make them unattractive to insects.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and prepare flowers for cross-pollination

steps.description: Five pistils were collected and fixed in formalin: acetic acid: ethanol in the ratio of 1:1:18.
steps.searchable_summary: fix pistils in formalin:acetic acid:ethanol solution

steps.description: After 3 days after pollination, when the pollen tube growth might have reached the base of the style, the pistils were fixed in the fixative.
steps.searchable_summary: fix pistils after 3 days of pollination

steps.description: From squash preparation of pistils washed in distilled water, the pollen tube growth along the style was monitored after the pistils have boiled in 5 per cent sodium sulphate to soften tissues and stained with 0.1 per cent aniline blue in 0.1N K3PO4.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pistils for microscopy by boiling and staining

steps.description: The pre-treated pistils were kept under Nikon microphot-Fx microscope with fluorescence attachment, illuminated with 200w high pressure UV lamp for examination.

The observations were taken with B (380-490nm) and or BG (650nm) excitation filters in combination with BA 520 or BA 530 barrier filter. Colour photographs were taken either with barrier filter (greenish yellow) or without barrier filter (blue background with bright white fluorescing pollen tube).
steps.searchable_summary: capture images using specific excitation and barrier filters

steps.description: Data were analyzed in completely randomized design and treatment means were compared with Duncans Multiple Range Test.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze data using randomized design and Duncans test
